1→package api
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"net/http"
     5→
     6→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9→)
    10→
    11→// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12→type Dependencies struct {
    13→	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14→	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15→	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16→	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17→	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18→	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19→	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20→	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21→	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22→	TodosStore            TodosStore
    23→	PublicURL             string
    24→	KeyStore              KeyStore
    25→	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    26→	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    27→	NoAuth                bool
    28→	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    29→	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    30→}
    31→
    32→// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    33→func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    34→	// Public chain: no auth required.
    35→	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    36→
    37→	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    38→	chain := publicChain
    39→	if !deps.NoAuth {
    40→		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    41→	}
    42→
    43→	// Projects (CRUD)
    44→	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    45→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    46→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    47→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    48→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    49→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    50→
    51→	// Sources (nested under projects)
    52→	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    53→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    54→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    55→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    56→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    57→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    58→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    59→
    60→	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    61→	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    62→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    63→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    64→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    65→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    66→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    67→
    68→	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    69→	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    70→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    71→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    72→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    73→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    74→
    75→	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    76→	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    77→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    78→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    79→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    80→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    81→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    82→
    83→	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    84→	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    85→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    86→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    87→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchCreate)))
    88→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/batch", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.BatchDelete)))
    89→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    90→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    91→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    92→
    93→	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    94→	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders(), deps.PublicURL)
    95→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    96→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    97→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    98→	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
    99→	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
   100→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
   101→
   102→	// Agent
   103→	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   104→	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   105→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   106→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   107→
   108→	// Todos
   109→	todos := NewTodosHandler(deps.TodosStore, deps.PublicURL)
   110→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.List)))
   111→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Get)))
   112→	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   113→	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   114→	mux.Handle("PATCH /api/v1/todos/{id}/reopen", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Reopen)))
   115→	// One-click endpoints for notification links (GET so they work as <a href>)
   116→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/acknowledge", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Acknowledge)))
   117→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/todos/{id}/resolve", chain(http.HandlerFunc(todos.Resolve)))
   118→
   119→	// Providers (metadata — static, no store needed)
   120→	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   121→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   122→
   123→	// Discovery (public — no auth, proxies external APIs)
   124→	discover := NewDiscoverHandler(deps.HTTPClient, "", "")
   125→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/github", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.GitHub)))
   126→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/discover/dockerhub", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(discover.DockerHub)))
   127→
   128→	// SSE events
   129→	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   130→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   131→
   132→	// Health (public — no auth middleware)
   133→	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   134→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   135→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   136→	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   137→}
   138→

1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  BatchSubscriptionInput,
    12→  BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput,
    13→  NotificationChannel,
    14→  ChannelInput,
    15→  ContextSource,
    16→  ContextSourceInput,
    17→  SemanticRelease,
    18→  AgentRun,
    19→  HealthStatus,
    20→  Stats,
    21→  TrendData,
    22→  DiscoverItem,
    23→  Todo,
    24→} from "./types";
    25→
    26→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    27→
    28→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    29→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    30→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    31→    ...init,
    32→  });
    33→  if (!res.ok) {
    34→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    35→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    36→  }
    37→  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    38→  return res.json();
    39→}
    40→
    41→// --- Projects ---
    42→
    43→export const projects = {
    44→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    45→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    46→  get: (id: string) =>
    47→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    48→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    49→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    50→      method: "POST",
    51→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    52→    }),
    53→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    54→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    55→      method: "PUT",
    56→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    57→    }),
    58→  delete: (id: string) =>
    59→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    60→};
    61→
    62→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    63→
    64→export const sources = {
    65→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    66→    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    67→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    68→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    69→      method: "POST",
    70→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    71→    }),
    72→  get: (id: string) =>
    73→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    74→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    75→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    76→      method: "PUT",
    77→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    78→    }),
    79→  delete: (id: string) =>
    80→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    81→  poll: (id: string) =>
    82→    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {
    83→      method: "POST",
    84→    }),
    85→};
    86→
    87→export const releases = {
    88→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    89→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    90→      `/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    91→    ),
    92→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
    93→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
    94→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    95→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    96→      `/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    97→    ),
    98→  get: (id: string) =>
    99→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
   100→};

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net/http"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"os/signal"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12→	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    17→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    18→)
    19→
    20→func main() {
    21→	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    22→	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    23→	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    24→	case "debug":
    25→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    26→	case "warn":
    27→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    28→	case "error":
    29→		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    30→	}
    31→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    32→
    33→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    34→	defer stop()
    35→
    36→	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    37→	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    38→	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    39→
    40→	// Database
    41→	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    42→	if err != nil {
    43→		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    44→		os.Exit(1)
    45→	}
    46→	defer pool.Close()
    47→
    48→	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    49→		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    50→		os.Exit(1)
    51→	}
    52→
    53→	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    54→	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    55→	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    56→	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    57→
    58→	// Register River workers
    59→	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    60→	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    61→	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    62→
    63→	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    64→	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    65→	// is restarted.
    66→	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    67→	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    68→	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    69→		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    70→	}
    71→	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    72→		Provider:      llmProvider,
    73→		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    74→		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    75→		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    76→		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    77→	}
    78→	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig, os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"))
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available — agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    81→	} else {
    82→		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    83→		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    84→		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
    88→	if err != nil {
    89→		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
    90→		os.Exit(1)
    91→	}
    92→
    93→	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
    94→	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
    95→	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
    96→
    97→	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
    98→		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
    99→		os.Exit(1)
   100→	}

100→	}
   101→
   102→	// Ingestion layer
   103→	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   104→	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   105→
   106→	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   107→	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   108→
   109→	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   110→
   111→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   112→
   113→	// Serve frontend static export if available
   114→	if info, err := os.Stat("web/out"); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   115→		api.RegisterFrontend(mux, "web/out")
   116→		slog.Info("serving frontend from web/out")
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	// Register all API v1 routes
   120→	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   121→		DB:                    pool,
   122→		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   123→		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   124→		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   125→		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   126→		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   127→		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   128→		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   129→		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   130→		TodosStore:            pgStore,
   131→		PublicURL:             os.Getenv("PUBLIC_URL"),
   132→		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   133→		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   134→		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   135→		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   136→		IngestionService:      svc,
   137→		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   138→	})
   139→
   140→	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   141→
   142→	// Start polling in background
   143→	go orch.Run(ctx)
   144→
   145→	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE bridge
   146→	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   147→
   148→	// Start HTTP server
   149→	go func() {
   150→		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   151→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   152→			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   153→		}
   154→	}()
   155→
   156→	<-ctx.Done()
   157→	slog.Info("shutting down")
   158→
   159→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   160→	defer cancel()
   161→	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   162→	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   163→}
   164→
   165→func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   166→	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   167→		return v
   168→	}
   169→	return fallback
   170→}
   171→

1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"fmt"
     6→
     7→	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
     8→	"google.golang.org/adk/model/gemini"
     9→	"google.golang.org/genai"
    10→
    11→	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// LLMConfig holds the configuration for creating an LLM model.
    15→type LLMConfig struct {
    16→	Provider string // "gemini" or "openai"
    17→	Model    string // e.g. "gemini-2.5-flash", "gpt-5.2"
    18→
    19→	// Gemini
    20→	GoogleAPIKey string
    21→
    22→	// OpenAI
    23→	OpenAIAPIKey  string
    24→	OpenAIBaseURL string // defaults to https://api.openai.com/v1
    25→}
    26→
    27→// NewLLMModel creates a model.LLM based on the configured provider.
    28→// wsConfig is optional and only applies to the OpenAI provider; it enables
    29→// the Responses API built-in web_search tool on the created model.
    30→func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig, wsConfig *oaimodel.WebSearch) (model.LLM, error) {
    31→	switch cfg.Provider {
    32→	case "gemini", "":
    33→		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
    34→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is required for gemini provider")
    35→		}
    36→		return gemini.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, &genai.ClientConfig{
    37→			APIKey: cfg.GoogleAPIKey,
    38→		})
    39→
    40→	case "openai":
    41→		return oaimodel.NewModel(ctx, cfg.Model, oaimodel.Config{
    42→			APIKey:  cfg.OpenAIAPIKey,
    43→			BaseURL: cfg.OpenAIBaseURL,
    44→		}, wsConfig)
    45→
    46→	default:
    47→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown LLM provider: %q (supported: gemini, openai)", cfg.Provider)
    48→	}
    49→}
    50→

content.text:
Now I have a deep understanding of the implementation patterns. Let me present the design section by section. 

---

## Design: GitHub Repo Onboarding (Dependency Scanner)

### Section 1: Backend — Scan Job & LLM-Powered Parsing

**New API endpoint**: `POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`  
- Accepts `{ "repo_url": "https://github.com/org/repo" }` (or just `"org/repo"`)
- Parses owner/repo, creates a "scan" record in a new `onboard_scans` table
- Enqueues a new River job `ScanDependenciesJobArgs`
- Returns immediately with `{ scan_id, status: "pending" }`

**New River job**: `scan_dependencies`  
- Worker fetches the repo tree via GitHub API (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/{default_branch}?recursive=1`)
- Identifies dependency-related files by name pattern (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle, etc.)
- Fetches contents of those files via GitHub API (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`)
- Sends all file contents to Gemini via ADK-Go with a structured prompt asking it to extract dependencies with: `name`, `version`, `ecosystem` (go, npm, python, etc.), and `upstream_repo` (best-guess GitHub repo URL or registry identifier)
- Stores parsed results in `onboard_scans.results` (JSONB)
- Updates scan status to `completed`
- Broadcasts SSE event `scan_complete`

**New table**: `onboard_scans`
```sql
CREATE TABLE onboard_scans (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',  -- pending, processing, completed, failed
    results JSONB,       -- array of {name, version, ecosystem, upstream_repo, ...}
    error TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);
```

**Polling endpoint**: `GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}` — returns the scan status and results.

**Apply endpoint**: `POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply`
- Accepts `{ "selections": [{ "dep_name": "...", "project_id": "existing-uuid" | null, "new_project_name": "..." }] }`
- For each selected dependency: creates project (if new) and source, then triggers first poll
- Returns created projects/sources

Does this backend section look right so far?

content.text:
### Section 2: Frontend — Onboarding Page

**New page**: `/onboard` — dedicated sidebar entry "Quick Onboard"

**Flow**:
1. **Input step**: Text field for GitHub repo URL + "Scan" button
2. **Scanning step**: Loading state while River job processes. Uses SSE or polling to wait for results.
3. **Results step**: Table of discovered dependencies with columns:
   - Checkbox (select/deselect)
   - Dependency name
   - Version (detected)
   - Ecosystem (Go, npm, Python, etc.)
   - Upstream source (GitHub repo URL)
   - Project assignment: dropdown — "Create new project" or select existing project
4. **Apply step**: "Track Selected" button. Creates projects/sources for selected dependencies. Shows success summary with links to created projects.

**Sidebar**: Add "Quick Onboard" entry, likely near the top since it's an entry point for new users.

content.text:
### Section 3: ADK-Go Agent for Dependency Parsing

**New agent**: A lightweight ADK-Go agent (not the full semantic release analyst) dedicated to dependency extraction.

**Prompt strategy**: The agent receives raw dependency file contents and returns structured JSON:

```
You are a dependency extraction agent. Given the contents of dependency/manifest files from a software project, extract all dependencies.

For each dependency, return:
- name: the package/library name
- version: the version constraint or pinned version
- ecosystem: one of "go", "npm", "pypi", "cargo", "rubygems", "maven", "gradle", "docker", "other"
- upstream_repo: your best guess at the canonical GitHub repository URL (e.g., "github.com/gorilla/mux")
- provider: the Changelogue provider to use for tracking — "github" for GitHub repos, "dockerhub" for Docker images

Return ONLY a JSON array. No explanations.
```

**Why a separate agent vs. raw Gemini call**: Using ADK-Go gives us structured tool use, retry logic, and consistency with the existing agent infrastructure. The agent is simple — no sub-agents or tools needed, just a single LLM call with structured output.

**Model**: Uses the same Gemini model configured for the system (reuses `internal/agent/model.go`).

Does this agent design look right?

content.text:
### Section 4: Error Handling & Edge Cases

- **Private repos**: Requires `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var (same as existing GitHub source). If token is missing/unauthorized, scan fails with clear error message.
- **No dependency files found**: LLM returns empty array → frontend shows "No dependencies detected" with option to manually add.
- **LLM parsing errors**: If Gemini returns malformed JSON, the worker retries (River's built-in retry mechanism, max 3 attempts). If all retries fail, scan status = `failed` with error message.
- **Duplicate sources**: The `UNIQUE(provider, repository)` constraint on `sources` table prevents duplicates. The apply endpoint checks for existing sources and skips/warns rather than erroring.
- **Rate limiting**: GitHub API rate limits (60/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr with token). For large repos with many dependency files, we batch file fetches. The scan job handles 429 responses with backoff.
- **Large repos**: The tree API returns the full file tree. We only fetch files matching known dependency file patterns, so even for repos with thousands of files, we only make a handful of content requests.

Does this error handling approach look right?

input.content:
# GitHub Repo Onboarding — Dependency Scanner

**Issue**: [#7 — Be able to connect to a GitHub repo and detect dependencies it has](https://github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/issues/7)

**Date**: 2026-03-14

## Overview

Add a "Quick Onboard" feature that lets users point Changelogue at a GitHub repository, automatically detect its dependencies using an LLM, and selectively create tracked projects/sources for those dependencies.

## Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Ecosystem scope | Multi-ecosystem via LLM | Gemini parses any manifest format without per-ecosystem parsers |
| Repo access | GitHub API | Lighter than cloning; fetches only needed files |
| Project mapping | User picks per dependency | Flexible — user chooses existing project or creates new |
| LLM provider | Gemini via ADK-Go | Already integrated for semantic releases |
| UX entry point | Dedicated `/onboard` page | Clear onboarding flow, separate from project management |
| Backend pattern | Async River job | Handles large repos; matches existing architecture |

## Architecture

### Data Flow

```
User enters repo URL
  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scan
    → River job: scan_dependencies
      → GitHub API: fetch repo tree
      → GitHub API: fetch dependency file contents
      → ADK-Go agent: parse dependencies via Gemini
      → Store results in onboard_scans table
      → SSE broadcast: scan_complete
  → Frontend polls/SSE for results
  → User selects dependencies to track
  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply
    → Create projects + sources
    → Trigger first poll for each source
```

### New Database Table

```sql
CREATE TABLE onboard_scans (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    results JSONB,
    error TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);
```

`results` schema (JSONB array):
```json
[
  {
    "name": "gorilla/mux",
    "version": "v1.8.1",
    "ecosystem": "go",
    "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
    "provider": "github"
  }
]
```

### New API Endpoints

**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`**
- Request: `{ "repo_url": "https://github.com/org/repo" }` (or `"org/repo"`)
- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "pending", "repo_url": "org/repo" } }`

**`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`**
- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "completed", "repo_url": "...", "results": [...], "completed_at": "..." } }`

**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply`**
- Request:
```json
{
  "selections": [
    {
      "dep_name": "gorilla/mux",
      "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
      "provider": "github",
      "project_id": "existing-uuid-or-null",
      "new_project_name": "gorilla-mux"
    }
  ]
}
```
- Response: `{ "data": { "created_projects": [...], "created_sources": [...], "skipped": [...] } }`

### New River Job

```go
type ScanDependenciesJobArgs struct {
    ScanID string `json:"scan_id"`
}

func (ScanDependenciesJobArgs) Kind() string { return "scan_dependencies" }
```

Worker steps:
1. Parse repo owner/name from `repo_url`
2. Fetch repo tree via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/{default_branch}?recursive=1`
3. Filter tree entries for known dependency file patterns (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle, etc.)
4. Fetch contents of matched files via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`
5. Invoke ADK-Go dependency extraction agent with file contents
6. Parse structured JSON response, store in `onboard_scans.results`
7. Update status to `completed`, broadcast SSE event

### ADK-Go Dependency Extraction Agent

A lightweight agent — single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools.

**Prompt**:
```
You are a dependency extraction agent. Given the contents of dependency/manifest
files from a software project, extract all dependencies.

For each dependency, return:
- name: the package/library name
- version: the version constraint or pinned version
- ecosystem: one of "go", "npm", "pypi", "cargo", "rubygems", "maven", "gradle", "docker", "other"
- upstream_repo: your best guess at the canonical GitHub repository URL (e.g., "github.com/gorilla/mux")
- provider: the Changelogue provider to use for tracking — "github" for GitHub repos, "dockerhub" for Docker images

Return ONLY a JSON array. No explanations.
```

Reuses model configuration from `internal/agent/model.go`.

## Frontend

### New Page: `/onboard`

**Sidebar entry**: "Quick Onboard" — positioned near the top for discoverability.

**Step 1 — Input**: Text field for GitHub repo URL + "Scan" button.

**Step 2 — Scanning**: Loading state with spinner. Uses SSE or polling (`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`) to wait for results.

**Step 3 — Results**: Table with columns:
- Checkbox (select/deselect, all selected by default)
- Dependency name
- Version (detected)
- Ecosystem badge (Go, npm, Python, etc.)
- Upstream source (GitHub repo URL)
- Project assignment: dropdown — "Create new project" or select existing project

**Step 4 — Apply**: "Track Selected" button. Shows success summary with links to created projects.

### Frontend API Client Additions

```typescript
export const onboard = {
  scan: (repoUrl: string) =>
    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>("/onboard/scan", {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({ repo_url: repoUrl }),
    }),
  getScan: (id: string) =>
    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}`),
  apply: (id: string, selections: OnboardSelection[]) =>
    request<ApiResponse<OnboardApplyResult>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}/apply`, {
      method: "POST",
      body: JSON.stringify({ selections }),
    }),
};
```

## Error Handling

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| Private repo without token | Scan fails with "GitHub API returned 404. Set GITHUB_TOKEN for private repos." |
| No dependency files found | LLM returns empty array → UI shows "No dependencies detected" |
| LLM returns malformed JSON | River retries (max 3 attempts); if all fail, status = `failed` with error |
| Duplicate source (provider+repo) | Apply endpoint skips with warning in `skipped` array |
| GitHub rate limit (429) | Worker retries with River's backoff; surfaces error after max retries |
| Large repo tree | Only fetches files matching dependency file name patterns |

## Testing

- **Unit tests**: Scan worker with mocked GitHub API + mocked ADK-Go agent
- **Unit tests**: Apply endpoint — creates projects/sources correctly, handles duplicates
- **Integration test**: End-to-end scan of a known public repo (e.g., `sentioxyz/changelogue` itself)
- **Frontend**: Manual testing of the onboard flow

## Files to Create/Modify

### New Files
- `internal/onboard/scanner.go` — GitHub API file fetcher + tree walker
- `internal/onboard/agent.go` — ADK-Go dependency extraction agent
- `internal/onboard/worker.go` — River job worker
- `internal/queue/scan_job.go` — Job args definition (or add to existing `jobs.go`)
- `internal/api/onboard.go` — API handlers (scan, get scan, apply)
- `web/app/onboard/page.tsx` — Onboarding page
- `web/lib/api/types.ts` — New types (OnboardScan, OnboardSelection, etc.)

### Modified Files
- `internal/db/migrations.go` — Add `onboard_scans` table
- `internal/api/server.go` — Register new routes
- `internal/api/pgstore.go` — Add store methods for onboard_scans
- `cmd/server/main.go` — Register scan worker with River
- `web/lib/api/client.ts` — Add onboard API functions
- `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` — Add "Quick Onboard" entry

1→package models
     2→

     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→type Release struct {
     9→	ID         string          `json:"id"`
    10→	SourceID   string          `json:"source_id"`
    11→	Version    string          `json:"version"`
    12→	RawData    json.RawMessage `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    13→	ReleasedAt *time.Time      `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    14→	CreatedAt  time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
    15→
    16→	// Enriched fields from JOINed sources/projects tables.
    17→	ProjectID   string `json:"project_id,omitempty"`
    18→	ProjectName string `json:"project_name,omitempty"`
    19→	Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
    20→	Repository  string `json:"repository,omitempty"`
    21→	Excluded    bool   `json:"excluded"` // true when filtered out by source version filters
    22→
    23→	// Enriched fields from LEFT JOINed semantic_releases table.
    24→	SemanticReleaseID      string `json:"semantic_release_id,omitempty"`
    25→	SemanticReleaseStatus  string `json:"semantic_release_status,omitempty"`
    26→	SemanticReleaseUrgency string `json:"semantic_release_urgency,omitempty"`
    27→}

content.content:
1→# GitHub Repo Onboarding — Dependency Scanner
     2→
     3→**Issue**: [#7 — Be able to connect to a GitHub repo and detect dependencies it has](https://github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/issues/7)
     4→
     5→**Date**: 2026-03-14
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→Add a "Quick Onboard" feature that lets users point Changelogue at a GitHub repository, automatically detect its dependencies using an LLM, and selectively create tracked projects/sources for those dependencies.
    10→
    11→## Decisions
    12→
    13→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    14→|----------|--------|-----------|
    15→| Ecosystem scope | Multi-ecosystem via LLM | Gemini parses any manifest format without per-ecosystem parsers |
    16→| Repo access | GitHub API | Lighter than cloning; fetches only needed files |
    17→| Project mapping | User picks per dependency | Flexible — user chooses existing project or creates new |
    18→| LLM provider | Gemini via ADK-Go | Already integrated for semantic releases |
    19→| UX entry point | Dedicated `/onboard` page | Clear onboarding flow, separate from project management |
    20→| Backend pattern | Async River job | Handles large repos; matches existing architecture |
    21→
    22→## Architecture
    23→
    24→### Data Flow
    25→
    26→```
    27→User enters repo URL
    28→  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scan
    29→    → River job: scan_dependencies
    30→      → GitHub API: fetch repo tree
    31→      → GitHub API: fetch dependency file contents
    32→      → ADK-Go agent: parse dependencies via Gemini
    33→      → Store results in onboard_scans table
    34→      → SSE broadcast: scan_complete
    35→  → Frontend polls/SSE for results
    36→  → User selects dependencies to track
    37→  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply
    38→    → Create projects + sources
    39→    → Trigger first poll for each source
    40→```
    41→
    42→### New Database Table
    43→
    44→```sql
    45→CREATE TABLE onboard_scans (
    46→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    47→    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    48→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    49→    results JSONB,
    50→    error TEXT,
    51→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    52→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
    53→);
    54→```
    55→
    56→`results` schema (JSONB array):
    57→```json
    58→[
    59→  {
    60→    "name": "gorilla/mux",
    61→    "version": "v1.8.1",
    62→    "ecosystem": "go",
    63→    "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
    64→    "provider": "github"
    65→  }
    66→]
    67→```
    68→
    69→### New API Endpoints
    70→
    71→**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`**
    72→- Request: `{ "repo_url": "https://github.com/org/repo" }` (or `"org/repo"`)
    73→- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "pending", "repo_url": "org/repo" } }`
    74→
    75→**`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`**
    76→- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "completed", "repo_url": "...", "results": [...], "completed_at": "..." } }`
    77→
    78→**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply`**
    79→- Request:
    80→```json
    81→{
    82→  "selections": [
    83→    {
    84→      "dep_name": "gorilla/mux",
    85→      "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
    86→      "provider": "github",
    87→      "project_id": "existing-uuid-or-null",
    88→      "new_project_name": "gorilla-mux"
    89→    }
    90→  ]
    91→}
    92→```
    93→- Response: `{ "data": { "created_projects": [...], "created_sources": [...], "skipped": [...] } }`
    94→
    95→### New River Job
    96→
    97→```go
    98→type ScanDependenciesJobArgs struct {
    99→    ScanID string `json:"scan_id"`
   100→}
   101→
   102→func (ScanDependenciesJobArgs) Kind() string { return "scan_dependencies" }
   103→```
   104→
   105→Worker steps:
   106→1. Parse repo owner/name from `repo_url`
   107→2. Fetch repo tree via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/{default_branch}?recursive=1`
   108→3. Filter tree entries for known dependency file patterns (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle, etc.)
   109→4. Fetch contents of matched files via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`
   110→5. Invoke ADK-Go dependency extraction agent with file contents
   111→6. Parse structured JSON response, store in `onboard_scans.results`
   112→7. Update status to `completed`, broadcast SSE event
   113→
   114→### ADK-Go Dependency Extraction Agent
   115→
   116→A lightweight agent — single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools.
   117→
   118→**Prompt**:
   119→```
   120→You are a dependency extraction agent. Given the contents of dependency/manifest
   121→files from a software project, extract all dependencies.
   122→
   123→For each dependency, return:
   124→- name: the package/library name
   125→- version: the version constraint or pinned version
   126→- ecosystem: one of "go", "npm", "pypi", "cargo", "rubygems", "maven", "gradle", "docker", "other"
   127→- upstream_repo: your best guess at the canonical GitHub repository URL (e.g., "github.com/gorilla/mux")
   128→- provider: the Changelogue provider to use for tracking — "github" for GitHub repos, "dockerhub" for Docker images
   129→
   130→Return ONLY a JSON array. No explanations.
   131→```
   132→
   133→Reuses model configuration from `internal/agent/model.go`.
   134→
   135→## Frontend
   136→
   137→### New Page: `/onboard`
   138→
   139→**Sidebar entry**: "Quick Onboard" — positioned near the top for discoverability.
   140→
   141→**Step 1 — Input**: Text field for GitHub repo URL + "Scan" button.
   142→
   143→**Step 2 — Scanning**: Loading state with spinner. Uses SSE or polling (`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`) to wait for results.
   144→
   145→**Step 3 — Results**: Table with columns:
   146→- Checkbox (select/deselect, all selected by default)
   147→- Dependency name
   148→- Version (detected)
   149→- Ecosystem badge (Go, npm, Python, etc.)
   150→- Upstream source (GitHub repo URL)
   151→- Project assignment: dropdown — "Create new project" or select existing project
   152→
   153→**Step 4 — Apply**: "Track Selected" button. Shows success summary with links to created projects.
   154→
   155→### Frontend API Client Additions
   156→
   157→```typescript
   158→export const onboard = {
   159→  scan: (repoUrl: string) =>
   160→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>("/onboard/scan", {
   161→      method: "POST",
   162→      body: JSON.stringify({ repo_url: repoUrl }),
   163→    }),
   164→  getScan: (id: string) =>
   165→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}`),
   166→  apply: (id: string, selections: OnboardSelection[]) =>
   167→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardApplyResult>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}/apply`, {
   168→      method: "POST",
   169→      body: JSON.stringify({ selections }),
   170→    }),
   171→};
   172→```
   173→
   174→## Error Handling
   175→
   176→| Scenario | Behavior |
   177→|----------|----------|
   178→| Private repo without token | Scan fails with "GitHub API returned 404. Set GITHUB_TOKEN for private repos." |
   179→| No dependency files found | LLM returns empty array → UI shows "No dependencies detected" |
   180→| LLM returns malformed JSON | River retries (max 3 attempts); if all fail, status = `failed` with error |
   181→| Duplicate source (provider+repo) | Apply endpoint skips with warning in `skipped` array |
   182→| GitHub rate limit (429) | Worker retries with River's backoff; surfaces error after max retries |
   183→| Large repo tree | Only fetches files matching dependency file name patterns |
   184→
   185→## Testing
   186→
   187→- **Unit tests**: Scan worker with mocked GitHub API + mocked ADK-Go agent
   188→- **Unit tests**: Apply endpoint — creates projects/sources correctly, handles duplicates
   189→- **Integration test**: End-to-end scan of a known public repo (e.g., `sentioxyz/changelogue` itself)
   190→- **Frontend**: Manual testing of the onboard flow
   191→
   192→## Files to Create/Modify
   193→
   194→### New Files
   195→- `internal/onboard/scanner.go` — GitHub API file fetcher + tree walker
   196→- `internal/onboard/agent.go` — ADK-Go dependency extraction agent
   197→- `internal/onboard/worker.go` — River job worker
   198→- `internal/queue/scan_job.go` — Job args definition (or add to existing `jobs.go`)
   199→- `internal/api/onboard.go` — API handlers (scan, get scan, apply)
   200→- `web/app/onboard/page.tsx` — Onboarding page
   201→- `web/lib/api/types.ts` — New types (OnboardScan, OnboardSelection, etc.)
   202→
   203→### Modified Files
   204→- `internal/db/migrations.go` — Add `onboard_scans` table
   205→- `internal/api/server.go` — Register new routes
   206→- `internal/api/pgstore.go` — Add store methods for onboard_scans
   207→- `cmd/server/main.go` — Register scan worker with River
   208→- `web/lib/api/client.ts` — Add onboard API functions
   209→- `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` — Add "Quick Onboard" entry
   210→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→# Architecture: Changelogue
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Changelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.
     6→
     7→## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure
     8→
     9→* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
    10→* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
    11→* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
    12→* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
    13→* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
    14→* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.
    15→
    16→
    17→* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
    18→* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.
    19→
    20→## 2. System Design & Abstractions
    21→
    22→The system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:
    23→
    24→1. **Ingestion Layer** — Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
    25→2. **Notification Routing** — River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
    26→3. **Agent Layer** — ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
    27→4. **Routing & Output** — Notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks) deliver alerts via `Sender`.
    28→
    29→A **Project** is the central domain entity — it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.
    30→
    31→```mermaid
    32→graph LR
    33→    %% Data Flow
    34→    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
    35→        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
    36→        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    37→    end
    38→
    39→    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
    40→        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    41→        I_Git(GitHub Atom Feed) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
    45→        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    46→    end
    47→
    48→    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
    49→        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
    50→        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Slack(Slack)
    51→        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Discord(Discord)
    52→        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Email(Email)
    53→        NotifyWorker --> |Sender| O_Hook(Webhooks)
    54→    end
    55→
    56→    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
    57→        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
    58→        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
    59→        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
    60→        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    61→    end
    62→
    63→```
    64→
    65→### Entity Relationship Model
    66→
    67→```mermaid
    68→erDiagram
    69→    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    70→    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    71→    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    72→    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    73→    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    74→    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    75→    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    76→    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    77→    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    78→    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"
    79→
    80→    projects {
    81→        uuid id PK
    82→        string name UK
    83→        text agent_prompt
    84→        jsonb agent_rules
    85→    }
    86→    sources {
    87→        uuid id PK
    88→        uuid project_id FK
    89→        string provider
    90→        string repository UK
    91→        boolean enabled
    92→    }
    93→    releases {
    94→        uuid id PK
    95→        uuid source_id FK
    96→        string version
    97→        jsonb raw_data
    98→    }
    99→    semantic_releases {
   100→        uuid id PK
   101→        uuid project_id FK
   102→        string version
   103→        jsonb report
   104→        string status
   105→    }
   106→    agent_runs {
   107→        uuid id PK
   108→        uuid project_id FK
   109→        uuid semantic_release_id FK
   110→        string trigger
   111→        string status
   112→    }
   113→    subscriptions {
   114→        uuid id PK
   115→        uuid channel_id FK
   116→        string type
   117→        uuid source_id FK
   118→        uuid project_id FK
   119→    }
   120→    notification_channels {
   121→        uuid id PK
   122→        string type
   123→        string name
   124→        jsonb config
   125→    }
   126→```
   127→
   128→### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)
   129→
   130→Components do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:
   131→
   132→* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
   133→* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
   134→* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
   135→* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` → `sources` → `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).
   136→
   137→### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)
   138→
   139→All external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.
   140→
   141→* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database.
   142→* `Sender`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   143→
   144→### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard
   145→
   146→The Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:
   147→
   148→* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels, context sources — all manageable through the API.
   149→* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not directly writable via the API — they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
   150→* **Source Operations:** Manual poll trigger (`POST /sources/{id}/poll`) and channel test notifications (`POST /channels/{id}/test`).
   151→* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
   152→* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. `NO_AUTH=true` disables auth for development.
   153→* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket (10 rps, 20 burst) with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.
   154→* **Stats & Trends:** Dashboard stats and time-bucketed release count trends via `/stats` and `/stats/trend`.
   155→
   156→See [API.md](API.md) for the full endpoint reference.
   157→
   158→### 2.4 Notification Routing
   159→
   160→When a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:
   161→
   162→1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
   163→2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `Sender`.
   164→3. Checks the project's agent rules — if the release matches (major bump, security patch, version pattern), enqueues an `agent_run` job automatically.
   165→
   166→### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)
   167→
   168→For project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:
   169→
   170→1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
   171→2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
   172→3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
   173→4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, download commands, urgency, recommendation).
   174→
   175→Agent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`, `wait_for_all_sources`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.
   176→
   177→**LLM Provider Support:**
   178→
   179→The agent supports multiple LLM backends via the `LLM_PROVIDER` environment variable:
   180→* **Gemini** (default) — Uses `GOOGLE_API_KEY` with ADK-Go's native Gemini model integration.
   181→* **OpenAI** — Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `OPENAI_BASE_URL` via an OpenAI-compatible adapter (`internal/agent/openai/`).
   182→
   183→**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**
   184→
   185→* **`tools.go`** — Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
   186→* **`orchestrator.go`** — `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with the configured LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
   187→* **`worker.go`** — `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
   188→* **Graceful degradation** — If no LLM API key is set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.
   189→
   190→### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)
   191→
   192→For deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:
   193→
   194→* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
   195→* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
   196→* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
   197→This will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.
   198→
   199→## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event
   200→
   201→1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied — filtered versions are discarded immediately.
   202→2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue a `notify_release` River job.
   203→3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks).
   204→4. **Agent Rule Check:** The same notification worker evaluates the project's `agent_rules` against the new release version. If criteria match (major bump, security patch, version pattern), it enqueues an `agent_run` River job.
   205→5. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** A River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (subject, risk level, changelog summary, status checks, urgency, recommendation).
   206→6. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
   207→7. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report.
   208→
   209→## 4. Directory Structure
   210→
   211→```text
   212→/changelogue
   213→├── cmd/
   214→│   ├── server/          # Main Go application entry point
   215→│   └── agent/           # Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project
   216→├── internal/
   217→│   ├── api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
   218→│   ├── ingestion/       # Polling workers (IIngestionSource: Docker Hub, GitHub Atom)
   219→│   ├── agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
   220→│   │   └── openai/      # OpenAI-compatible LLM provider adapter
   221→│   ├── routing/         # Notification routing worker and channel implementations (Sender)
   222→│   ├── queue/           # River queue job definitions (NotifyJobArgs, AgentJobArgs)
   223→│   ├── db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
   224→│   └── models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
   225→├── web/                 # Next.js frontend application
   226→│   ├── app/             # App Router pages (dashboard, projects, releases, channels, etc.)
   227→│   ├── components/      # React components organized by domain
   228→│   └── lib/             # Client utilities and API wrappers
   229→├── scripts/             # Integration test harness
   230→├── docs/                # Design documents and plans
   231→└── go.mod
   232→```

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
