content.content:
1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"google.golang.org/genai"
    13→
    14→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent"
    15→	"google.golang.org/adk/agent/llmagent"
    16→	"google.golang.org/adk/model"
    17→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin"
    18→	"google.golang.org/adk/plugin/loggingplugin"
    19→	"google.golang.org/adk/runner"
    20→	"google.golang.org/adk/session"
    21→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
    22→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/geminitool"
    23→
    24→	oaimodel "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent/openai"
    25→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    26→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    27→)
    28→
    29→// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    30→// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    31→// the resulting semantic release.
    32→type OrchestratorStore interface {
    33→	AgentDataStore
    34→	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    35→	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    36→	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    37→	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    38→	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    39→	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    40→	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    41→	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    42→	CreateSemanticReleaseTodo(ctx context.Context, semanticReleaseID string) (string, error)
    43→}
    44→
    45→const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    46→
    47→Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    48→
    49→Use the available tools to:
    50→1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    51→2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    52→3. Check binary/image availability.
    53→4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    54→5. Always search the web for supplementary context about this release
    55→   (community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues, migration guides, direct download links).
    56→
    57→CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    58→Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.
    59→
    60→For download_links: ONLY include URLs where the user can actually download the software — direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) and the GitHub/project release page. Do NOT include CVE links, security advisory URLs, blog posts, documentation, or any non-download URLs.
    61→
    62→The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    63→{
    64→  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    65→  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    66→  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    67→  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    68→  "changelog_summary": "summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    69→  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    70→  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15", "curl -L -o geth.tar.gz https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz"],
    71→  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    72→}`
    73→
    74→// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    75→// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    76→// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    77→func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    78→	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    79→	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    80→		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    81→	}
    82→	if version != "" {
    83→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    84→	} else {
    85→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
    86→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    90→	if err != nil {
    91→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    95→	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    96→	if err != nil {
    97→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
    98→	}
    99→
   100→	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   101→	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   102→		Name:        "data_agent",
   103→		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   104→		Model:       llmModel,
   105→		Tools:       functionTools,
   106→	})
   107→	if err != nil {
   108→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   109→	}
   110→
   111→	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   112→	var searchTool tool.Tool
   113→	var searchModel model.LLM
   114→	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   115→	case "openai":
   116→		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   117→		searchTool = *wsConfig
   118→		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   119→		if err != nil {
   120→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   121→		}
   122→	default: // gemini
   123→		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   124→		searchModel = llmModel
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	// Fetch project sources to give the search agent context about where releases come from.
   128→	sources, _, err := store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, project.ID, 1, 100)
   129→	if err != nil {
   130→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list project sources: %w", err)
   131→	}
   132→	var sourceLines []string
   133→	for _, s := range sources {
   134→		sourceLines = append(sourceLines, fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s", s.Provider, s.Repository))
   135→	}
   136→
   137→	searchInstruction := fmt.Sprintf(
   138→		"You are a web search assistant for the project %q. Always include the project name in your search queries to get relevant results. Focus on this specific project — ignore results for unrelated projects that happen to share the same version number.",
   139→		project.Name,
   140→	)
   141→	if len(sourceLines) > 0 {
   142→		searchInstruction += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nThis project's upstream sources are:\n%s\nUse these repository names and URLs to refine your search queries.", strings.Join(sourceLines, "\n"))
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   146→		Name:        "search_agent",
   147→		Description: "Search the web for context about a release — community sentiment, security advisories, adoption statistics, known issues, and migration guides. Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights.",
   148→		Instruction: searchInstruction,
   149→		Model:       searchModel,
   150→		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   151→	})
   152→	if err != nil {
   153→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   157→	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   158→		Name:        "release_analyst",
   159→		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   160→		Model:       llmModel,
   161→		Instruction: instruction,
   162→		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   163→			newTracedAgentTool(dataAgent),
   164→			newTracedAgentTool(searchAgent),
   165→		},
   166→	})
   167→}
   168→
   169→// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   170→// release matching the target version.
   171→func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   172→	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   173→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   174→	if err != nil {
   175→		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   176→	}
   177→	if len(sources) == 0 {
   178→		return true, nil
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	for _, src := range sources {
   182→		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   183→		if err != nil {
   184→			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   185→		}
   186→		if !has {
   187→			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   188→				"project_id", projectID,
   189→				"source_id", src.ID,
   190→				"version", version,
   191→			)
   192→			return false, nil
   193→		}
   194→	}
   195→	return true, nil
   196→}
   197→
   198→// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   199→// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   200→// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   201→type Orchestrator struct {
   202→	store     OrchestratorStore
   203→	llmConfig LLMConfig
   204→	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   205→}
   206→
   207→// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   208→// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   209→// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   210→func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   211→	switch cfg.Provider {
   212→	case "openai":
   213→		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   214→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   215→		}
   216→	default: // gemini
   217→		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   218→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   219→		}
   220→	}
   221→	return &Orchestrator{
   222→		store:     store,
   223→		llmConfig: cfg,
   224→		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   225→	}, nil
   226→}
   227→
   228→// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   229→//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   230→//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   231→//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   232→//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   233→//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   234→//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   235→//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   236→func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   237→	// Mark as running.
   238→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   239→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   240→	}
   241→
   242→	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   243→	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   244→	if err != nil {
   245→		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   246→		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   247→		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   248→			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   249→				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   250→		}
   251→		return err
   252→	}
   253→
   254→	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   255→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   256→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   257→	}
   258→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   259→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   260→	}
   261→
   262→	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   263→	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   264→
   265→	return nil
   266→}
   267→
   268→// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   269→// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   270→type agentResult struct {
   271→	semanticReleaseID string
   272→	version           string
   273→	reportText        string
   274→	projectName       string
   275→}
   276→
   277→// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   278→// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   279→func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   280→	// Load project.
   281→	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   282→	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   285→	}
   286→	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   287→
   288→	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   289→	version := run.Version
   290→	if version == "" {
   291→		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   292→		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   293→			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   294→		}
   295→	}
   296→
   297→	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   298→	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   299→		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   300→		"version", version)
   301→	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   302→	if err != nil {
   303→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   304→	}
   305→
   306→	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   307→	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   308→	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   309→		AppName: "changelogue",
   310→		UserID:  "system",
   311→	})
   312→	if err != nil {
   313→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   314→	}
   315→	sess := createResp.Session
   316→
   317→	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   318→	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   319→		AppName:        "changelogue",
   320→		Agent:          agentInstance,
   321→		SessionService: sessionService,
   322→		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   323→			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   324→			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   325→		},
   326→	})
   327→	if err != nil {
   328→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   329→	}
   330→
   331→	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   332→	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   333→	var userPrompt string
   334→	if version != "" {
   335→		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   336→	} else {
   337→		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   338→	}
   339→	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   340→
   341→	var finalText string
   342→	eventCount := 0
   343→	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   344→		if err != nil {
   345→			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   346→				"run_id", run.ID,
   347→				"event_count", eventCount,
   348→				"err", err,
   349→			)
   350→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   351→		}
   352→		eventCount++
   353→		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   354→			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   355→				if part.Text != "" {
   356→					finalText += part.Text
   357→				}
   358→			}
   359→		}
   360→	}
   361→	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   362→		"run_id", run.ID,
   363→		"event_count", eventCount,
   364→		"output_length", len(finalText),
   365→	)
   366→
   367→	if finalText == "" {
   368→		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   369→			"run_id", run.ID,
   370→			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   371→			"event_count", eventCount,
   372→		)
   373→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   374→	}
   375→
   376→	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   377→	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   378→	if err != nil {
   379→		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   380→			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   381→		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   382→		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   383→			Summary: finalText,
   384→		}
   385→	}
   386→
   387→	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   388→	if err != nil {
   389→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   390→	}
   391→
   392→	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   393→	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   394→	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   395→	if err != nil {
   396→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   397→	}
   398→	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   399→	for _, r := range releases {
   400→		if r.Version == version {
   401→			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   402→		}
   403→	}
   404→
   405→	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   406→	srVersion := version
   407→	if srVersion == "" {
   408→		srVersion = "unknown"
   409→		if len(releases) > 0 {
   410→			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   411→		}
   412→	}
   413→
   414→	now := time.Now()
   415→	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   416→		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   417→		Version:     srVersion,
   418→		Report:      reportJSON,
   419→		Status:      "completed",
   420→		CompletedAt: &now,
   421→	}
   422→
   423→	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   424→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   425→	}
   426→
   427→	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   428→		"run_id", run.ID,
   429→		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   430→		"version", sr.Version,
   431→	)
   432→
   433→	return &agentResult{
   434→		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   435→		version:           srVersion,
   436→		reportText:        finalText,
   437→		projectName:       project.Name,
   438→	}, nil
   439→}
   440→
   441→// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   442→// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   443→func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   444→	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   445→	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   446→		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   447→		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   448→		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   449→	}
   450→}
   451→
   452→// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   453→// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   454→// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   455→func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   456→	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   457→	if err != nil {
   458→		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   459→			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   460→		return
   461→	}
   462→	if len(subs) == 0 {
   463→		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   464→			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   465→		return
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	senders := defaultSenders()
   469→
   470→	// Create a TODO for this semantic release.
   471→	todoID, todoErr := o.store.CreateSemanticReleaseTodo(ctx, result.semanticReleaseID)
   472→	if todoErr != nil {
   473→		slog.Error("create semantic release todo failed",
   474→			"semantic_release_id", result.semanticReleaseID, "err", todoErr)
   475→	}
   476→
   477→	msg := routing.Notification{
   478→		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   479→		Body:        result.reportText,
   480→		Version:     result.version,
   481→		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   482→	}
   483→	if todoID != "" {
   484→		msg.TodoID = todoID
   485→		msg.PublicURL = o.publicURL
   486→	}
   487→
   488→	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   489→	if o.publicURL != "" {
   490→		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   491→	}
   492→
   493→	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   494→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   495→	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   496→		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   497→		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   498→		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	for _, sub := range subs {
   502→		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   503→		if err != nil {
   504→			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   505→				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   506→			continue
   507→		}
   508→
   509→		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   510→		if !ok {
   511→			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   512→			continue
   513→		}
   514→
   515→		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   516→			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   517→				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   518→		} else {
   519→			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   520→				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   521→		}
   522→	}
   523→}
   524→
   525→// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   526→// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   527→func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   528→	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   529→	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   530→	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   531→		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   532→		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   533→			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   534→		}
   535→		// Remove closing fence.
   536→		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   537→			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   538→		}
   539→		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   540→	}
   541→
   542→	var report models.SemanticReport
   543→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   544→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   545→	}
   546→
   547→	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   548→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   549→	}
   550→
   551→	return &report, nil
   552→}
   553→

You are a spec document reviewer. Review the design spec at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-github-repo-onboarding-design.md` for quality, completeness, and technical accuracy.

Context: This is a design for adding GitHub repo onboarding to Changelogue — a release intelligence platform. The feature lets users scan a GitHub repo's dependency files, uses an LLM (Gemini via ADK-Go) to extract dependencies, and lets users selectively create tracked projects/sources.

The codebase uses:
- Go backend with PostgreSQL + River job queue
- ADK-Go (Google's Agent Development Kit) for LLM integration
- Next.js frontend with Tailwind CSS
- SSE for real-time updates
- Existing models: Project, Source, Release, SemanticRelease

Please evaluate:
1. **Completeness**: Are all necessary components addressed? Any gaps?
2. **Technical accuracy**: Do the proposed patterns match Go/Next.js/ADK-Go best practices?
3. **Consistency**: Does it follow existing codebase patterns (check migrations, API handlers, River jobs)?
4. **Clarity**: Is the spec clear enough for implementation without ambiguity?
5. **Edge cases**: Are error scenarios adequately covered?
6. **YAGNI**: Is there unnecessary complexity that should be removed?

Read the spec file and the key codebase files (models, API handlers, migrations, queue jobs) to cross-reference. Return a structured review with:
- **Status**: "Approved" or "Issues Found"
- **Issues**: List any problems with severity (Critical/Minor)
- **Suggestions**: Optional improvements

content.content:
1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"time"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    11→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    13→)
    14→
    15→// AgentWorker is a River worker that processes AgentJobArgs by running the
    16→// LLM agent orchestrator. When a job is dequeued, it loads the corresponding
    17→// agent run from the store and delegates to Orchestrator.RunAgent.
    18→type AgentWorker struct {
    19→	river.WorkerDefaults[queue.AgentJobArgs]
    20→	orchestrator *Orchestrator
    21→	store        OrchestratorStore
    22→}
    23→
    24→// NewAgentWorker creates a new AgentWorker backed by the given orchestrator
    25→// and store.
    26→func NewAgentWorker(orchestrator *Orchestrator, store OrchestratorStore) *AgentWorker {
    27→	return &AgentWorker{
    28→		orchestrator: orchestrator,
    29→		store:        store,
    30→	}
    31→}
    32→
    33→// Timeout overrides River's default 60-second job timeout. Agent runs involve
    34→// multiple LLM round-trips (root agent + sub-agents), each of which can take
    35→// 10-30 seconds, so we allow 5 minutes total.
    36→func (w *AgentWorker) Timeout(_ *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) time.Duration {
    37→	return 5 * time.Minute
    38→}
    39→
    40→// Work processes a single AgentJobArgs job. It loads the agent run from the
    41→// database and runs the LLM agent through the orchestrator. If the project
    42→// has WaitForAllSources enabled and not all sources have the target version,
    43→// the job is snoozed for 5 minutes via river.JobSnooze.
    44→func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    45→	slog.Info("agent worker picked up job",
    46→		"job_id", job.ID,
    47→		"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    48→		"attempt", job.Attempt,
    49→	)
    50→
    51→	run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    52→	if err != nil {
    53→		slog.Error("agent worker failed to load agent run",
    54→			"job_id", job.ID,
    55→			"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    56→			"err", err,
    57→		)
    58→		return fmt.Errorf("get agent run: %w", err)
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	// Check multi-source waiting: if the run has a version and the project
    62→	// has WaitForAllSources enabled, verify all sources have reported a
    63→	// release for the target version before running the agent.
    64→	if run.Version != "" {
    65→		project, err := w.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
    66→		if err != nil {
    67→			slog.Error("agent worker failed to load project",
    68→				"job_id", job.ID,
    69→				"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    70→				"err", err,
    71→			)
    72→			return fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
    73→		}
    74→
    75→		var rules models.AgentRules
    76→		if len(project.AgentRules) > 0 {
    77→			if err := json.Unmarshal(project.AgentRules, &rules); err != nil {
    78→				slog.Warn("agent worker: failed to unmarshal agent rules, proceeding without wait",
    79→					"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
    80→			}
    81→		}
    82→
    83→		if rules.WaitForAllSources {
    84→			ready, err := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
    85→			if err != nil {
    86→				slog.Error("agent worker: error checking source readiness",
    87→					"job_id", job.ID,
    88→					"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    89→					"version", run.Version,
    90→					"err", err,
    91→				)
    92→				return fmt.Errorf("check all sources ready: %w", err)
    93→			}
    94→			if !ready {
    95→				// After maxWaitAttempts (12 × 5min = ~1 hour), proceed
    96→				// with partial data rather than waiting forever.
    97→				const maxWaitAttempts = 12
    98→				if job.Attempt >= maxWaitAttempts {
    99→					slog.Warn("agent worker: max wait attempts reached, proceeding with partial data",
   100→						"job_id", job.ID,
   101→						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   102→						"version", run.Version,
   103→						"attempts", job.Attempt,
   104→					)
   105→				} else {
   106→					slog.Info("agent worker: not all sources ready, snoozing",
   107→						"job_id", job.ID,
   108→						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   109→						"version", run.Version,
   110→						"attempt", job.Attempt,
   111→					)
   112→					if err := w.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "waiting"); err != nil {
   113→						slog.Error("failed to set waiting status", "err", err)
   114→					}
   115→					return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)
   116→				}
   117→			}
   118→		}
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	slog.Info("agent worker starting run",
   122→		"job_id", job.ID,
   123→		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   124→		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   125→		"trigger", run.Trigger,
   126→	)
   127→
   128→	if err := w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run); err != nil {
   129→		slog.Error("agent worker run failed",
   130→			"job_id", job.ID,
   131→			"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   132→			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   133→			"err", err,
   134→		)
   135→		return err
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	slog.Info("agent worker run completed",
   139→		"job_id", job.ID,
   140→		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   141→		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   142→	)
   143→	return nil
   144→}
   145→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→// web/lib/api/client.ts
     2→import type {
     3→  ApiResponse,
     4→  Project,
     5→  ProjectInput,
     6→  Release,
     7→  Source,
     8→  SourceInput,
     9→  Subscription,
    10→  SubscriptionInput,
    11→  BatchSubscriptionInput,
    12→  BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput,
    13→  NotificationChannel,
    14→  ChannelInput,
    15→  ContextSource,
    16→  ContextSourceInput,
    17→  SemanticRelease,
    18→  AgentRun,
    19→  HealthStatus,
    20→  Stats,
    21→  TrendData,
    22→  DiscoverItem,
    23→  Todo,
    24→} from "./types";
    25→
    26→const BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "/api/v1";
    27→
    28→async function request<T>(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<T> {
    29→  const res = await fetch(`${BASE}${path}`, {
    30→    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...init?.headers },
    31→    ...init,
    32→  });
    33→  if (!res.ok) {
    34→    const body = await res.json().catch(() => null);
    35→    throw new Error(body?.error?.message ?? `Request failed: ${res.status}`);
    36→  }
    37→  if (res.status === 204) return null as T;
    38→  return res.json();
    39→}
    40→
    41→// --- Projects ---
    42→
    43→export const projects = {
    44→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
    45→    request<ApiResponse<Project[]>>(`/projects?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
    46→  get: (id: string) =>
    47→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`),
    48→  create: (input: ProjectInput) =>
    49→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>("/projects", {
    50→      method: "POST",
    51→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    52→    }),
    53→  update: (id: string, input: ProjectInput) =>
    54→    request<ApiResponse<Project>>(`/projects/${id}`, {
    55→      method: "PUT",
    56→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    57→    }),
    58→  delete: (id: string) =>
    59→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/projects/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    60→};
    61→
    62→// --- Sources (nested under projects) ---
    63→
    64→export const sources = {
    65→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
    66→    request<ApiResponse<Source[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources?page=${page}`),
    67→  create: (projectId: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    68→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/projects/${projectId}/sources`, {
    69→      method: "POST",
    70→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    71→    }),
    72→  get: (id: string) =>
    73→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`),
    74→  update: (id: string, input: SourceInput) =>
    75→    request<ApiResponse<Source>>(`/sources/${id}`, {
    76→      method: "PUT",
    77→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
    78→    }),
    79→  delete: (id: string) =>
    80→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
    81→  poll: (id: string) =>
    82→    request<ApiResponse<{ new_releases: number }>>(`/sources/${id}/poll`, {
    83→      method: "POST",
    84→    }),
    85→};
    86→
    87→export const releases = {
    88→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    89→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    90→      `/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    91→    ),
    92→  listBySource: (sourceId: string, page = 1) =>
    93→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/sources/${sourceId}/releases?page=${page}`),
    94→  listByProject: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, includeExcluded = false) =>
    95→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(
    96→      `/projects/${projectId}/releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}${includeExcluded ? '&include_excluded=true' : ''}`
    97→    ),
    98→  get: (id: string) =>
    99→    request<ApiResponse<Release>>(`/releases/${id}`),
   100→};
   101→
   102→// --- Context Sources ---
   103→
   104→export const contextSources = {
   105→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   106→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources?page=${page}`),
   107→  create: (projectId: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   108→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/projects/${projectId}/context-sources`, {
   109→      method: "POST",
   110→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   111→    }),
   112→  get: (id: string) =>
   113→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`),
   114→  update: (id: string, input: ContextSourceInput) =>
   115→    request<ApiResponse<ContextSource>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, {
   116→      method: "PUT",
   117→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   118→    }),
   119→  delete: (id: string) =>
   120→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/context-sources/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   121→};
   122→
   123→// --- Semantic Releases ---
   124→
   125→export const semanticReleases = {
   126→  listAll: (page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   127→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   128→  list: (projectId: string, page = 1, perPage = 25) =>
   129→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/semantic-releases?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   130→  get: (id: string) =>
   131→    request<ApiResponse<SemanticRelease>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`),
   132→  delete: (id: string) =>
   133→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   134→  getSources: (id: string) =>
   135→    request<ApiResponse<Release[]>>(`/semantic-releases/${id}/sources`),
   136→};
   137→
   138→// --- Agent ---
   139→
   140→export const agent = {
   141→  triggerRun: (projectId: string, version?: string) =>
   142→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/run`, {
   143→      method: "POST",
   144→      body: JSON.stringify({ trigger: "test", version: version || undefined }),
   145→    }),
   146→  listRuns: (projectId: string, page = 1) =>
   147→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun[]>>(`/projects/${projectId}/agent/runs?page=${page}`),
   148→  getRun: (id: string) =>
   149→    request<ApiResponse<AgentRun>>(`/agent-runs/${id}`),
   150→};
   151→
   152→// --- Subscriptions ---
   153→
   154→export const subscriptions = {
   155→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   156→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>(`/subscriptions?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   157→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`),
   158→  create: (input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   159→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>("/subscriptions", {
   160→      method: "POST",
   161→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   162→    }),
   163→  batchCreate: (input: BatchSubscriptionInput) =>
   164→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription[]>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   165→      method: "POST",
   166→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   167→    }),
   168→  update: (id: string, input: SubscriptionInput) =>
   169→    request<ApiResponse<Subscription>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, {
   170→      method: "PUT",
   171→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   172→    }),
   173→  delete: (id: string) =>
   174→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/subscriptions/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   175→  batchDelete: (input: BatchDeleteSubscriptionInput) =>
   176→    request<ApiResponse<null>>("/subscriptions/batch", {
   177→      method: "DELETE",
   178→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   179→    }),
   180→};
   181→
   182→// --- Notification Channels ---
   183→
   184→export const channels = {
   185→  list: (page = 1, perPage = 100) =>
   186→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel[]>>(`/channels?page=${page}&per_page=${perPage}`),
   187→  get: (id: string) => request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`),
   188→  create: (input: ChannelInput) =>
   189→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>("/channels", {
   190→      method: "POST",
   191→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   192→    }),
   193→  update: (id: string, input: ChannelInput) =>
   194→    request<ApiResponse<NotificationChannel>>(`/channels/${id}`, {
   195→      method: "PUT",
   196→      body: JSON.stringify(input),
   197→    }),
   198→  delete: (id: string) =>
   199→    request<ApiResponse<null>>(`/channels/${id}`, { method: "DELETE" }),
   200→  test: (id: string) =>
   201→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/channels/${id}/test`, {
   202→      method: "POST",
   203→    }),
   204→};
   205→
   206→// --- Todos ---
   207→
   208→export const todos = {
   209→  list: (status?: string, page = 1, perPage = 25, aggregated = false) => {
   210→    const params = new URLSearchParams({ page: String(page), per_page: String(perPage) });
   211→    if (status) params.set("status", status);
   212→    if (aggregated) params.set("aggregated", "true");
   213→    return request<ApiResponse<Todo[]>>(`/todos?${params}`);
   214→  },
   215→  get: (id: string) =>
   216→    request<ApiResponse<Todo>>(`/todos/${id}`),
   217→  acknowledge: (id: string, cascade = false) =>
   218→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/acknowledge${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   219→  resolve: (id: string, cascade = false) =>
   220→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/resolve${cascade ? '?cascade=true' : ''}`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   221→  reopen: (id: string) =>
   222→    request<ApiResponse<{ status: string }>>(`/todos/${id}/reopen`, { method: "PATCH" }),
   223→};
   224→
   225→// --- System ---
   226→
   227→export const system = {
   228→  health: () => request<HealthStatus>("/health"),
   229→  stats: () => request<ApiResponse<Stats>>("/stats"),
   230→  trend: (granularity: "daily" | "weekly" | "monthly" = "daily", days = 7) =>
   231→    request<ApiResponse<TrendData>>(`/stats/trend?granularity=${granularity}&days=${days}`),
   232→};
   233→
   234→// --- Discovery ---
   235→
   236→export const discover = {
   237→  github: (params?: { q?: string; language?: string }) => {
   238→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   239→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   240→    if (params?.language) search.set("language", params.language);
   241→    const qs = search.toString();
   242→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/github${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   243→  },
   244→  dockerhub: (params?: { q?: string }) => {
   245→    const search = new URLSearchParams();
   246→    if (params?.q) search.set("q", params.q);
   247→    const qs = search.toString();
   248→    return request<ApiResponse<DiscoverItem[]>>(`/discover/dockerhub${qs ? `?${qs}` : ""}`);
   249→  },
   250→};
   251→

1→package queue
     2→
     3→import "github.com/riverqueue/river"
     4→
     5→// NotifyJobArgs is enqueued when a new source release is detected.
     6→// The worker sends notifications to source-level subscribers.
     7→type NotifyJobArgs struct {
     8→	ReleaseID string `json:"release_id"`
     9→	SourceID  string `json:"source_id"`
    10→}
    11→
    12→func (NotifyJobArgs) Kind() string { return "notify_release" }
    13→
    14→var _ river.JobArgs = NotifyJobArgs{}
    15→
    16→// AgentJobArgs is enqueued when an agent run is triggered.
    17→// The worker runs the LLM agent to produce a semantic release.
    18→type AgentJobArgs struct {
    19→	AgentRunID string `json:"agent_run_id"`
    20→	ProjectID  string `json:"project_id"`
    21→	Version    string `json:"version"`
    22→}
    23→
    24→func (AgentJobArgs) Kind() string { return "agent_run" }
    25→
    26→var _ river.JobArgs = AgentJobArgs{}
    27→

content.content:
1→package queue
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
     5→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     6→	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
     7→	"github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// NewRiverClient creates a River client backed by the given pgx pool.
    11→// Pass nil workers to create an insert-only client (no job processing).
    12→func NewRiverClient(pool *pgxpool.Pool, workers *river.Workers) (*river.Client[pgx.Tx], error) {
    13→	config := &river.Config{}
    14→	if workers != nil {
    15→		config.Workers = workers
    16→		config.Queues = map[string]river.QueueConfig{
    17→			river.QueueDefault: {MaxWorkers: 100},
    18→		}
    19→	}
    20→	return river.NewClient(riverpgxv5.New(pool), config)
    21→}
    22→

1→package ingestion
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"time"
    10→)
    11→
    12→const defaultGitHubAPIURL = "https://api.github.com"
    13→
    14→// ghRelease represents a single release from the GitHub REST API.
    15→type ghRelease struct {
    16→	TagName     string `json:"tag_name"`
    17→	Body        string `json:"body"`
    18→	HTMLURL     string `json:"html_url"`
    19→	PublishedAt string `json:"published_at"`
    20→	Prerelease  bool   `json:"prerelease"`
    21→	Draft       bool   `json:"draft"`
    22→}
    23→
    24→// GitHubSource polls the GitHub REST API for repository releases.
    25→type GitHubSource struct {
    26→	client     *http.Client
    27→	repository string
    28→	baseURL    string
    29→	sourceID   string
    30→}
    31→
    32→func NewGitHubSource(client *http.Client, repository string, sourceID string) *GitHubSource {
    33→	return &GitHubSource{
    34→		client:     client,
    35→		repository: repository,
    36→		baseURL:    defaultGitHubAPIURL,
    37→		sourceID:   sourceID,
    38→	}
    39→}
    40→
    41→func (s *GitHubSource) Name() string    { return "github" }
    42→func (s *GitHubSource) SourceID() string { return s.sourceID }
    43→
    44→func (s *GitHubSource) FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) {
    45→	url := fmt.Sprintf("%s/repos/%s/releases?per_page=30", s.baseURL, s.repository)
    46→
    47→	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
    48→	if err != nil {
    49→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create request: %w", err)
    50→	}

content.text:
I have now reviewed all the relevant codebase files. Here is my structured review.

---

## Spec Review: GitHub Repo Onboarding -- Dependency Scanner

**Spec file:** `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-github-repo-onboarding-design.md`

**Status: Issues Found**

Overall this is a well-structured spec that follows the project's conventions and architectural patterns. The data flow is clear, the River job pattern matches existing usage, and the ADK-Go agent approach is consistent with what already exists in `internal/agent/`. However, I found several issues that should be addressed before implementation.

---

### What Was Done Well

- The data flow diagram is clear and easy to follow -- the scan/poll/apply lifecycle makes sense.
- The decision to use a River job for async scanning is architecturally consistent with `AgentJobArgs` and `NotifyJobArgs` in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`.
- The ADK-Go agent approach for dependency extraction reuses existing infrastructure (model configuration from `internal/agent/model.go`). Describing it as "a lightweight agent -- single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools" is a clear simplification from the full orchestrator pattern.
- The error handling table covers the key failure modes.
- The file list (new/modified) is specific and actionable.
- The API client additions in TypeScript follow the exact pattern used in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`.

---

### Critical Issues

**C1. Missing `GITHUB_TOKEN` configuration and authentication story**

The spec mentions "Private repo without token" as an error case but never describes how `GITHUB_TOKEN` is provided to the scanner. Looking at the existing GitHub ingestion source in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/github.go` (line 9), it reads from `os.Getenv`. The spec should explicitly state:
- Where the token comes from (environment variable, like the existing pattern).
- Whether the user can provide a per-scan token via the API request body (useful if users scan repos that require different credentials).
- Whether the GitHub tree API and contents API calls share the same auth mechanism.

Without this, the implementer will have to guess.

**C2. Missing `updated_at` column on `onboard_scans` table**

Every existing table in the schema at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` uses `created_at` and most use `updated_at`. The `onboard_scans` table only has `created_at` and `completed_at`. While `completed_at` covers the terminal state, the scan also transitions through `pending` -> `scanning` -> `completed`/`failed`. There is no way to track when the scan started processing. Consider adding either `updated_at` or `started_at` for observability parity with `agent_runs` (which has `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `created_at`).

**C3. The `apply` endpoint creates projects and sources but has no transactional guarantee specified**

The spec says the apply endpoint creates projects and sources, but does not specify that these operations should happen in a single database transaction. Looking at the existing pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go` lines 423-425, `CreateSemanticRelease` operates within a transaction. The apply endpoint needs the same treatment -- if creating the 5th source fails, the first 4 should not be left as orphans. The spec should explicitly state "single transaction" for the apply batch.

---

### Important Issues (Should Fix)

**I1. ADK-Go agent description says "single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools" but this is misleading**

The spec says the dependency extraction agent is "A lightweight agent -- single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools." However, looking at the existing agent pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go`, creating an ADK-Go `llmagent` involves `runner.Runner`, `session.InMemoryService`, etc. This is significant overhead for what is essentially a structured output extraction task. The spec should clarify either:

(a) This uses the full ADK-Go agent stack (runner, session, etc.) -- in which case "no tools" is fine but "lightweight" is misleading about the code complexity, or
(b) This is a direct `genai.GenerateContent` call to Gemini without ADK-Go orchestration -- which would be simpler and more appropriate for a single-turn, no-tool extraction.

Option (b) is recommended for a task that has no tools, no sub-agents, and no multi-turn conversation. Using ADK-Go for this adds unnecessary complexity (YAGNI). A direct Gemini API call with structured output/JSON mode would be simpler and faster.

**I2. The `ScanDependenciesJobArgs` struct only contains `ScanID` but the Worker needs the repo URL**

In `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`, the existing `AgentJobArgs` includes `AgentRunID`, `ProjectID`, and `Version`. The scan job only has `ScanID`, which means the worker must query the database to get the repo URL. This is fine (and matches the agent pattern where the worker loads the run from the store), but should be explicitly stated in the worker steps. Currently step 1 says "Parse repo owner/name from `repo_url`" without saying where `repo_url` comes from.

**I3. No `OnboardStore` interface defined**

Every existing feature domain defines a store interface in the handler file. For example:
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/todos.go` lines 11-17 define `TodosStore`
- The agent handler uses `AgentStore`

The spec lists store methods being added to `pgstore.go` but does not define the `OnboardStore` interface that the `OnboardHandler` will depend on. This should be specified to maintain the existing pattern of interface-driven dependency injection.

**I4. The apply request schema allows both `project_id` (existing) and `new_project_name` (create new) but does not specify validation rules**

What happens if both are provided? What if neither is provided? What if `new_project_name` conflicts with an existing project name (the `projects` table has `UNIQUE(name)`)? These edge cases need specification.

**I5. Provider value inconsistency in the LLM prompt**

The spec's LLM prompt says to return provider as `"github"` or `"dockerhub"`. But looking at the existing sources table UNIQUE constraint in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` line 37, the `provider` column stores values like `"github"` and `"dockerhub"`. However, the ingestion layer also supports `"gitlab"`, `"ecr_public"` (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/gitlab.go` and `ecr_public.go`). The LLM prompt should either:
- Include all supported providers, or
- Default unsupported ecosystems to `"github"` with an explanation.

**I6. Missing SSE event type for scan completion**

The spec says "SSE broadcast: scan_complete" but does not define the PostgreSQL trigger or the event format. Existing SSE events are defined via PL/pgSQL triggers in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` lines 134-159, broadcasting on the `release_events` channel. The spec should specify:
- Whether to reuse the `release_events` channel or create a new one.
- The trigger function and event payload format.
- Whether the frontend `SSEEvent` type union in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/types.ts` needs to be extended.

**I7. No mention of `GITHUB_TOKEN` for the tree/contents API calls**

The existing `GitHubSource` in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/github.go` sets an `Authorization` header when `GITHUB_TOKEN` is available. The spec's scanner needs the same mechanism for the tree and contents API calls, but this is not mentioned in the worker steps.

---

### Minor Issues / Suggestions

**S1. Sidebar placement mismatch**

The spec says "Quick Onboard" should be "positioned near the top for discoverability." Looking at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` lines 18-25, the current order is Dashboard, Projects, Todo, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions. The spec should specify exactly where in this list the entry goes (e.g., after Dashboard, or after Projects).

**S2. Consider scan deduplication**

There is no unique constraint preventing the same repo from being scanned multiple times simultaneously. Consider adding `UNIQUE(repo_url) WHERE status = 'pending' OR status = 'scanning'` as a partial unique index, or at least checking for active scans in the POST handler.

**S3. The `results` JSONB field should specify `provider` more precisely**

The results schema includes a `provider` field per dependency, but the LLM will be guessing. For Go dependencies, they are almost always on GitHub. For npm packages, the "upstream repo" is often on GitHub but the registry is npm. The spec should clarify whether `provider` means "where to poll for releases" (registry-centric) or "where the source code lives" (repo-centric). Looking at the existing `sources` table, `provider` means the polling source (github, dockerhub, gitlab, ecr_public), so the LLM prompt should be explicit about this distinction.

**S4. File creation under `internal/onboard/` is a new package**

The spec creates `internal/onboard/scanner.go`, `agent.go`, and `worker.go`. This is a new Go package, which is fine, but note that the existing agent code lives in `internal/agent/` and the existing queue jobs live in `internal/queue/`. Consider whether the scan job args should go in `internal/queue/jobs.go` (to collocate with `NotifyJobArgs` and `AgentJobArgs`) rather than a separate `internal/queue/scan_job.go`. The spec already mentions this as an alternative ("or add to existing `jobs.go`") -- I recommend the latter for consistency.

**S5. The apply endpoint triggers first poll but does not specify how**

The spec says "Trigger first poll for each source" in the apply step, but does not specify whether this enqueues a River job, calls the ingestion service directly, or something else. Looking at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 58, there is a `POST /sources/{id}/poll` endpoint that calls `sources.FetchReleases`. The apply handler should likely use the `IngestionService` directly, which should be stated.

**S6. Testing section could be more specific**

The testing section says "Unit tests: Scan worker with mocked GitHub API + mocked ADK-Go agent" but does not mention testing the apply endpoint's duplicate detection logic or the transaction rollback scenario. Given the complexity of the apply endpoint (creating multiple projects + sources + triggering polls), the test plan should be more detailed.

---

### Recommendations

1. **Address all Critical (C1-C3) and Important (I1-I7) issues** before beginning implementation. The critical items can cause implementation ambiguity or architectural inconsistency.

2. **Strongly consider using direct Gemini API calls instead of ADK-Go** for the dependency extraction (I1). This is a single-turn structured output task -- ADK-Go adds runner/session/plugin overhead for zero benefit here.

3. **Add the `OnboardStore` interface** to the spec (I3) to maintain consistency with every other handler in the codebase.

4. **Specify the transaction boundary** for the apply endpoint (C3) -- this is the most complex part of the feature and is underspecified.

5. **Consolidate scan job args into `internal/queue/jobs.go`** (S4) to maintain the single-file convention for River job definitions.

content.text:
I have now reviewed all the relevant codebase files. Here is my structured review.

---

## Spec Review: GitHub Repo Onboarding -- Dependency Scanner

**Spec file:** `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-github-repo-onboarding-design.md`

**Status: Issues Found**

Overall this is a well-structured spec that follows the project's conventions and architectural patterns. The data flow is clear, the River job pattern matches existing usage, and the ADK-Go agent approach is consistent with what already exists in `internal/agent/`. However, I found several issues that should be addressed before implementation.

---

### What Was Done Well

- The data flow diagram is clear and easy to follow -- the scan/poll/apply lifecycle makes sense.
- The decision to use a River job for async scanning is architecturally consistent with `AgentJobArgs` and `NotifyJobArgs` in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`.
- The ADK-Go agent approach for dependency extraction reuses existing infrastructure (model configuration from `internal/agent/model.go`). Describing it as "a lightweight agent -- single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools" is a clear simplification from the full orchestrator pattern.
- The error handling table covers the key failure modes.
- The file list (new/modified) is specific and actionable.
- The API client additions in TypeScript follow the exact pattern used in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/client.ts`.

---

### Critical Issues

**C1. Missing `GITHUB_TOKEN` configuration and authentication story**

The spec mentions "Private repo without token" as an error case but never describes how `GITHUB_TOKEN` is provided to the scanner. Looking at the existing GitHub ingestion source in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/github.go` (line 9), it reads from `os.Getenv`. The spec should explicitly state:
- Where the token comes from (environment variable, like the existing pattern).
- Whether the user can provide a per-scan token via the API request body (useful if users scan repos that require different credentials).
- Whether the GitHub tree API and contents API calls share the same auth mechanism.

Without this, the implementer will have to guess.

**C2. Missing `updated_at` column on `onboard_scans` table**

Every existing table in the schema at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` uses `created_at` and most use `updated_at`. The `onboard_scans` table only has `created_at` and `completed_at`. While `completed_at` covers the terminal state, the scan also transitions through `pending` -> `scanning` -> `completed`/`failed`. There is no way to track when the scan started processing. Consider adding either `updated_at` or `started_at` for observability parity with `agent_runs` (which has `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `created_at`).

**C3. The `apply` endpoint creates projects and sources but has no transactional guarantee specified**

The spec says the apply endpoint creates projects and sources, but does not specify that these operations should happen in a single database transaction. Looking at the existing pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go` lines 423-425, `CreateSemanticRelease` operates within a transaction. The apply endpoint needs the same treatment -- if creating the 5th source fails, the first 4 should not be left as orphans. The spec should explicitly state "single transaction" for the apply batch.

---

### Important Issues (Should Fix)

**I1. ADK-Go agent description says "single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools" but this is misleading**

The spec says the dependency extraction agent is "A lightweight agent -- single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools." However, looking at the existing agent pattern in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/agent/orchestrator.go`, creating an ADK-Go `llmagent` involves `runner.Runner`, `session.InMemoryService`, etc. This is significant overhead for what is essentially a structured output extraction task. The spec should clarify either:

(a) This uses the full ADK-Go agent stack (runner, session, etc.) -- in which case "no tools" is fine but "lightweight" is misleading about the code complexity, or
(b) This is a direct `genai.GenerateContent` call to Gemini without ADK-Go orchestration -- which would be simpler and more appropriate for a single-turn, no-tool extraction.

Option (b) is recommended for a task that has no tools, no sub-agents, and no multi-turn conversation. Using ADK-Go for this adds unnecessary complexity (YAGNI). A direct Gemini API call with structured output/JSON mode would be simpler and faster.

**I2. The `ScanDependenciesJobArgs` struct only contains `ScanID` but the Worker needs the repo URL**

In `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/queue/jobs.go`, the existing `AgentJobArgs` includes `AgentRunID`, `ProjectID`, and `Version`. The scan job only has `ScanID`, which means the worker must query the database to get the repo URL. This is fine (and matches the agent pattern where the worker loads the run from the store), but should be explicitly stated in the worker steps. Currently step 1 says "Parse repo owner/name from `repo_url`" without saying where `repo_url` comes from.

**I3. No `OnboardStore` interface defined**

Every existing feature domain defines a store interface in the handler file. For example:
- `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/todos.go` lines 11-17 define `TodosStore`
- The agent handler uses `AgentStore`

The spec lists store methods being added to `pgstore.go` but does not define the `OnboardStore` interface that the `OnboardHandler` will depend on. This should be specified to maintain the existing pattern of interface-driven dependency injection.

**I4. The apply request schema allows both `project_id` (existing) and `new_project_name` (create new) but does not specify validation rules**

What happens if both are provided? What if neither is provided? What if `new_project_name` conflicts with an existing project name (the `projects` table has `UNIQUE(name)`)? These edge cases need specification.

**I5. Provider value inconsistency in the LLM prompt**

The spec's LLM prompt says to return provider as `"github"` or `"dockerhub"`. But looking at the existing sources table UNIQUE constraint in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` line 37, the `provider` column stores values like `"github"` and `"dockerhub"`. However, the ingestion layer also supports `"gitlab"`, `"ecr_public"` (see `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/gitlab.go` and `ecr_public.go`). The LLM prompt should either:
- Include all supported providers, or
- Default unsupported ecosystems to `"github"` with an explanation.

**I6. Missing SSE event type for scan completion**

The spec says "SSE broadcast: scan_complete" but does not define the PostgreSQL trigger or the event format. Existing SSE events are defined via PL/pgSQL triggers in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/db/migrations.go` lines 134-159, broadcasting on the `release_events` channel. The spec should specify:
- Whether to reuse the `release_events` channel or create a new one.
- The trigger function and event payload format.
- Whether the frontend `SSEEvent` type union in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/lib/api/types.ts` needs to be extended.

**I7. No mention of `GITHUB_TOKEN` for the tree/contents API calls**

The existing `GitHubSource` in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/ingestion/github.go` sets an `Authorization` header when `GITHUB_TOKEN` is available. The spec's scanner needs the same mechanism for the tree and contents API calls, but this is not mentioned in the worker steps.

---

### Minor Issues / Suggestions

**S1. Sidebar placement mismatch**

The spec says "Quick Onboard" should be "positioned near the top for discoverability." Looking at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` lines 18-25, the current order is Dashboard, Projects, Todo, Releases, Channels, Subscriptions. The spec should specify exactly where in this list the entry goes (e.g., after Dashboard, or after Projects).

**S2. Consider scan deduplication**

There is no unique constraint preventing the same repo from being scanned multiple times simultaneously. Consider adding `UNIQUE(repo_url) WHERE status = 'pending' OR status = 'scanning'` as a partial unique index, or at least checking for active scans in the POST handler.

**S3. The `results` JSONB field should specify `provider` more precisely**

The results schema includes a `provider` field per dependency, but the LLM will be guessing. For Go dependencies, they are almost always on GitHub. For npm packages, the "upstream repo" is often on GitHub but the registry is npm. The spec should clarify whether `provider` means "where to poll for releases" (registry-centric) or "where the source code lives" (repo-centric). Looking at the existing `sources` table, `provider` means the polling source (github, dockerhub, gitlab, ecr_public), so the LLM prompt should be explicit about this distinction.

**S4. File creation under `internal/onboard/` is a new package**

The spec creates `internal/onboard/scanner.go`, `agent.go`, and `worker.go`. This is a new Go package, which is fine, but note that the existing agent code lives in `internal/agent/` and the existing queue jobs live in `internal/queue/`. Consider whether the scan job args should go in `internal/queue/jobs.go` (to collocate with `NotifyJobArgs` and `AgentJobArgs`) rather than a separate `internal/queue/scan_job.go`. The spec already mentions this as an alternative ("or add to existing `jobs.go`") -- I recommend the latter for consistency.

**S5. The apply endpoint triggers first poll but does not specify how**

The spec says "Trigger first poll for each source" in the apply step, but does not specify whether this enqueues a River job, calls the ingestion service directly, or something else. Looking at `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/api/server.go` line 58, there is a `POST /sources/{id}/poll` endpoint that calls `sources.FetchReleases`. The apply handler should likely use the `IngestionService` directly, which should be stated.

**S6. Testing section could be more specific**

The testing section says "Unit tests: Scan worker with mocked GitHub API + mocked ADK-Go agent" but does not mention testing the apply endpoint's duplicate detection logic or the transaction rollback scenario. Given the complexity of the apply endpoint (creating multiple projects + sources + triggering polls), the test plan should be more detailed.

---

### Recommendations

1. **Address all Critical (C1-C3) and Important (I1-I7) issues** before beginning implementation. The critical items can cause implementation ambiguity or architectural inconsistency.

2. **Strongly consider using direct Gemini API calls instead of ADK-Go** for the dependency extraction (I1). This is a single-turn structured output task -- ADK-Go adds runner/session/plugin overhead for zero benefit here.

3. **Add the `OnboardStore` interface** to the spec (I3) to maintain consistency with every other handler in the codebase.

4. **Specify the transaction boundary** for the apply endpoint (C3) -- this is the most complex part of the feature and is underspecified.

5. **Consolidate scan job args into `internal/queue/jobs.go`** (S4) to maintain the single-file convention for River job definitions.
Good review with actionable feedback. Let me update the spec to address the issues.

1→# GitHub Repo Onboarding — Dependency Scanner
     2→
     3→**Issue**: [#7 — Be able to connect to a GitHub repo and detect dependencies it has](https://github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/issues/7)
     4→
     5→**Date**: 2026-03-14
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→Add a "Quick Onboard" feature that lets users point Changelogue at a GitHub repository, automatically detect its dependencies using an LLM, and selectively create tracked projects/sources for those dependencies.
    10→
    11→## Decisions
    12→
    13→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    14→|----------|--------|-----------|
    15→| Ecosystem scope | Multi-ecosystem via LLM | Gemini parses any manifest format without per-ecosystem parsers |
    16→| Repo access | GitHub API | Lighter than cloning; fetches only needed files |
    17→| Project mapping | User picks per dependency | Flexible — user chooses existing project or creates new |
    18→| LLM provider | Gemini via ADK-Go | Already integrated for semantic releases |
    19→| UX entry point | Dedicated `/onboard` page | Clear onboarding flow, separate from project management |
    20→| Backend pattern | Async River job | Handles large repos; matches existing architecture |
    21→
    22→## Architecture
    23→
    24→### Data Flow
    25→
    26→```
    27→User enters repo URL
    28→  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scan
    29→    → River job: scan_dependencies
    30→      → GitHub API: fetch repo tree
    31→      → GitHub API: fetch dependency file contents
    32→      → ADK-Go agent: parse dependencies via Gemini
    33→      → Store results in onboard_scans table
    34→      → SSE broadcast: scan_complete
    35→  → Frontend polls/SSE for results
    36→  → User selects dependencies to track
    37→  → POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply
    38→    → Create projects + sources
    39→    → Trigger first poll for each source
    40→```
    41→
    42→### New Database Table
    43→
    44→```sql
    45→CREATE TABLE onboard_scans (
    46→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    47→    repo_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    48→    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
    49→    results JSONB,
    50→    error TEXT,
    51→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    52→    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
    53→);
    54→```
    55→
    56→`results` schema (JSONB array):
    57→```json
    58→[
    59→  {
    60→    "name": "gorilla/mux",
    61→    "version": "v1.8.1",
    62→    "ecosystem": "go",
    63→    "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
    64→    "provider": "github"
    65→  }
    66→]
    67→```
    68→
    69→### New API Endpoints
    70→
    71→**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scan`**
    72→- Request: `{ "repo_url": "https://github.com/org/repo" }` (or `"org/repo"`)
    73→- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "pending", "repo_url": "org/repo" } }`
    74→
    75→**`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`**
    76→- Response: `{ "data": { "id": "uuid", "status": "completed", "repo_url": "...", "results": [...], "completed_at": "..." } }`
    77→
    78→**`POST /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}/apply`**
    79→- Request:
    80→```json
    81→{
    82→  "selections": [
    83→    {
    84→      "dep_name": "gorilla/mux",
    85→      "upstream_repo": "github.com/gorilla/mux",
    86→      "provider": "github",
    87→      "project_id": "existing-uuid-or-null",
    88→      "new_project_name": "gorilla-mux"
    89→    }
    90→  ]
    91→}
    92→```
    93→- Response: `{ "data": { "created_projects": [...], "created_sources": [...], "skipped": [...] } }`
    94→
    95→### New River Job
    96→
    97→```go
    98→type ScanDependenciesJobArgs struct {
    99→    ScanID string `json:"scan_id"`
   100→}
   101→
   102→func (ScanDependenciesJobArgs) Kind() string { return "scan_dependencies" }
   103→```
   104→
   105→Worker steps:
   106→1. Parse repo owner/name from `repo_url`
   107→2. Fetch repo tree via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/trees/{default_branch}?recursive=1`
   108→3. Filter tree entries for known dependency file patterns (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle, etc.)
   109→4. Fetch contents of matched files via `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path}`
   110→5. Invoke ADK-Go dependency extraction agent with file contents
   111→6. Parse structured JSON response, store in `onboard_scans.results`
   112→7. Update status to `completed`, broadcast SSE event
   113→
   114→### ADK-Go Dependency Extraction Agent
   115→
   116→A lightweight agent — single LLM call, no sub-agents or tools.
   117→
   118→**Prompt**:
   119→```
   120→You are a dependency extraction agent. Given the contents of dependency/manifest
   121→files from a software project, extract all dependencies.
   122→
   123→For each dependency, return:
   124→- name: the package/library name
   125→- version: the version constraint or pinned version
   126→- ecosystem: one of "go", "npm", "pypi", "cargo", "rubygems", "maven", "gradle", "docker", "other"
   127→- upstream_repo: your best guess at the canonical GitHub repository URL (e.g., "github.com/gorilla/mux")
   128→- provider: the Changelogue provider to use for tracking — "github" for GitHub repos, "dockerhub" for Docker images
   129→
   130→Return ONLY a JSON array. No explanations.
   131→```
   132→
   133→Reuses model configuration from `internal/agent/model.go`.
   134→
   135→## Frontend
   136→
   137→### New Page: `/onboard`
   138→
   139→**Sidebar entry**: "Quick Onboard" — positioned near the top for discoverability.
   140→
   141→**Step 1 — Input**: Text field for GitHub repo URL + "Scan" button.
   142→
   143→**Step 2 — Scanning**: Loading state with spinner. Uses SSE or polling (`GET /api/v1/onboard/scans/{id}`) to wait for results.
   144→
   145→**Step 3 — Results**: Table with columns:
   146→- Checkbox (select/deselect, all selected by default)
   147→- Dependency name
   148→- Version (detected)
   149→- Ecosystem badge (Go, npm, Python, etc.)
   150→- Upstream source (GitHub repo URL)
   151→- Project assignment: dropdown — "Create new project" or select existing project
   152→
   153→**Step 4 — Apply**: "Track Selected" button. Shows success summary with links to created projects.
   154→
   155→### Frontend API Client Additions
   156→
   157→```typescript
   158→export const onboard = {
   159→  scan: (repoUrl: string) =>
   160→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>("/onboard/scan", {
   161→      method: "POST",
   162→      body: JSON.stringify({ repo_url: repoUrl }),
   163→    }),
   164→  getScan: (id: string) =>
   165→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardScan>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}`),
   166→  apply: (id: string, selections: OnboardSelection[]) =>
   167→    request<ApiResponse<OnboardApplyResult>>(`/onboard/scans/${id}/apply`, {
   168→      method: "POST",
   169→      body: JSON.stringify({ selections }),
   170→    }),
   171→};
   172→```
   173→
   174→## Error Handling
   175→
   176→| Scenario | Behavior |
   177→|----------|----------|
   178→| Private repo without token | Scan fails with "GitHub API returned 404. Set GITHUB_TOKEN for private repos." |
   179→| No dependency files found | LLM returns empty array → UI shows "No dependencies detected" |
   180→| LLM returns malformed JSON | River retries (max 3 attempts); if all fail, status = `failed` with error |
   181→| Duplicate source (provider+repo) | Apply endpoint skips with warning in `skipped` array |
   182→| GitHub rate limit (429) | Worker retries with River's backoff; surfaces error after max retries |
   183→| Large repo tree | Only fetches files matching dependency file name patterns |
   184→
   185→## Testing
   186→
   187→- **Unit tests**: Scan worker with mocked GitHub API + mocked ADK-Go agent
   188→- **Unit tests**: Apply endpoint — creates projects/sources correctly, handles duplicates
   189→- **Integration test**: End-to-end scan of a known public repo (e.g., `sentioxyz/changelogue` itself)
   190→- **Frontend**: Manual testing of the onboard flow
   191→
   192→## Files to Create/Modify
   193→
   194→### New Files
   195→- `internal/onboard/scanner.go` — GitHub API file fetcher + tree walker
   196→- `internal/onboard/agent.go` — ADK-Go dependency extraction agent
   197→- `internal/onboard/worker.go` — River job worker
   198→- `internal/queue/scan_job.go` — Job args definition (or add to existing `jobs.go`)
   199→- `internal/api/onboard.go` — API handlers (scan, get scan, apply)
   200→- `web/app/onboard/page.tsx` — Onboarding page
   201→- `web/lib/api/types.ts` — New types (OnboardScan, OnboardSelection, etc.)
   202→
   203→### Modified Files
   204→- `internal/db/migrations.go` — Add `onboard_scans` table
   205→- `internal/api/server.go` — Register new routes
   206→- `internal/api/pgstore.go` — Add store methods for onboard_scans
   207→- `cmd/server/main.go` — Register scan worker with River
   208→- `web/lib/api/client.ts` — Add onboard API functions
   209→- `web/components/layout/sidebar.tsx` — Add "Quick Onboard" entry
   210→
